content:
Another strong symbol in the story is the scarecrow. When Phoenix sees it, she mistakes it for a ghost and is frightened at first. But when she realizes what it is, she laughs and says, "I ought to be shut up for good" (Welty). The scarecrow represents the fears and struggles she faces on her journey. At first, it appears threatening, much like the many dangers she encounters. But when she understands it’s harmless, she laughs, showing her ability to overcome fear and keep going. This moment reflects Phoenix’s strengths she has been through so much in life that even when she is scared, she finds a way to move forward.

Through these symbols, Welty emphasizes the themes of perseverance and sacrifice. Phoenix’s journey is not just about getting medicine, it represents her love and dedication to her grandson. She faces physical hardships, fear, and exhaustion, yet she never gives up. A Worn Path is a powerful story about the strength of love and the endurance of the human spirit.


from Week 5 Discussion
